description_public: A polygenic estimate related to bitter taste sensitivity (ptc-like) variation in the general population.

CC.notes: This genotype is strongly associated with being able to taste certain bitter compounds (like PTC/PROP) more intensely, which may also influence how some vegetables and bitter foods taste.

CG.notes: This genotype is usually linked with moderate sensitivity to these bitter compounds, so some foods may taste somewhat bitter without being extremely intense.

GG.notes: This genotype is associated with a reduced ability to taste certain bitter compounds, so some people experience these foods as milder or less bitter overall.

evidence.title: Placeholder evidence for Bitter taste sensitivity (PTC-like)
evidence.quote: This genotype is strongly associated with being able to taste certain bitter compounds (like PTC/PROP) more intensely, which may also influence how some vegetables and bitter foods taste.
